solution.p: First we square each side to obtain a quadratic equation. Then we can rearrange that into standard form.
Now we can identify a={1}\text{,} b={-17}\text{,} and c={16}\text{,} and apply the quadratic formula.
The only possible solutions are 1 and 16\text{.} But if we check the original equation, we find that 1 is a soluiton, but 16 is not. So the solution set is {\left\{1\right\}}\text{.}
